posts.title: IBD School and Workplace Accommodations: ImproveCareNow Accommodations Toolkit
posts.description: 504 plans, college accommodations, and workplace flexibility for Crohn's and colitis. Guide to the ImproveCareNow Accommodations Toolkit with download links.
posts.share: ICN Accommodations Toolkit for IBD: school, work, and daily life support.
posts.icn_source_title: Accommodations for Students (K-12 & Higher Ed), Employees & Everyday Life
posts.body:
Many people with Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis need accommodations at school, college, or work: extra bathroom access, flexible deadlines, modified attendance, or quiet spaces to manage symptoms. The ImproveCareNow Accommodations Toolkit is a co-produced patient resource that walks families through real-world options.

What the toolkit covers

  • Frequent absences, extended sick leave, and medical appointments
  • Trouble concentrating when symptoms flare
  • K-12 and higher-education accommodation planning
  • Employee accommodations and everyday-life flexibility

ICN describes accommodations as extra support, flexibility, or modifications that help individuals living with IBD address barriers, not special treatment, but practical adjustments so education and work remain possible.

Downloads on ImproveCareNow

On the ICN tools page you can download:

  • Accommodations Toolkit (full resource, English)
  • Accommodations Highlight (shorter English summary)
  • Accommodations Highlight (Spanish / español)

Pair this toolkit with IBDPal's school and workplace rights overview and patient guide on 504 and ADA basics.

How to use it at your care center

Bring the highlight sheet to your gastroenterology visit and ask for a letter supporting your accommodation request. School nurses, disability services offices, and HR departments often need documentation from your medical team.

Related: teens and school hub, high school survival guide, college with IBD.

posts.title: College With IBD: ImproveCareNow College & IBD Handbook
posts.description: Choosing a school, dorms, dining halls, and disability services with IBD. Overview of the ImproveCareNow College & IBD Toolkit.
posts.share: ICN College & IBD Toolkit: navigating higher education with Crohn's or colitis.
posts.body:
Transitioning to college with a chronic illness can feel overwhelming. The ImproveCareNow College & IBD Toolkit is a handbook co-produced by patients, parents, and clinicians to make the process less stressful.

Who it is for

High school students planning ahead, current college students managing flares away from home, and family members supporting the transition.

Topics inside the handbook

  • Choosing a school that fits your health and support needs
  • Preparing medically and practically before move-in
  • Surviving classes when fatigue or urgency spikes
  • Dorms, roommates, and shared bathrooms
  • Dining halls, meal plans, and safe food strategies
  • Disability services and accommodation letters

Get the toolkit

Download the College & IBD Toolkit and a shorter College & IBD Highlight from the ICN tools page linked below. Highlights are useful to share with school counselors or college health offices.

See also IBDPal's college with IBD article, high school guide, and ICN accommodations toolkit overview.

posts.title: Caregiver Coping With Pediatric IBD: ImproveCareNow Family Resource
posts.description: Parent and caregiver mental health, sibling impact, and stress with pediatric Crohn's or colitis. Guide to the ICN Caregiver Coping Resource.
posts.share: ICN Caregiver Coping Resource for families of children with IBD.
posts.body:
Parenting a child with Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis brings joy and real challenges. The ImproveCareNow Caregiver Coping Resource is a 160+ page co-produced guide from ICN's Parent/Family Advisory Council, social workers, psychologists, and A-LiNK Connections.

What caregivers will find

  • Navigating joys and challenges of parenting a child with a chronic condition
  • How stress impacts health for caregivers and children
  • Impact of chronic illness on siblings
  • Practical coping strategies from other caregivers and psychosocial professionals

Content is compiled by caregivers of children with IBD and autoimmune liver disease, alongside psychologists and a medical social worker who work with pediatric GI families.

Access the resource

Visit the ICN tools page for the full Caregiver Coping Resource and a one-page printable handout you can share at your care center.

Related IBDPal pages: pediatric caregivers hub, partner and caregiver article, living with IBD as a family, pediatric help guide.

posts.title: Finding a Mental Health Provider for Youth With IBD: ICN Guide
posts.description: How to find therapy for children and teens with Crohn's or colitis. Overview of ImproveCareNow's four-page mental health provider guide.
posts.share: ICN guide to finding mental health support for youth with IBD.
posts.icn_source_title: Finding A Mental Health Provider Guide
posts.body:
Comprehensive IBD care increasingly includes mental health. The ImproveCareNow Finding a Mental Health Provider Guide is a four-page co-produced resource from ICN's Social Worker and Psychologist (SWAP) group.

What the guide explains

  • Why addressing mental health is part of whole-person IBD care
  • How to find and vet a mental health provider
  • What to expect during counseling sessions
  • Working with your GI team alongside therapy

English and Spanish PDF versions are available on the ICN tools page.

When to use this guide

Consider it if your child has persistent anxiety about flares, school avoidance, low mood, sleep disruption, or difficulty adjusting to diagnosis or medications. Pair with IBDPal's depression and anxiety article and stress coping strategies.

Related: stress and anxiety guide, pediatric caregivers, teens and social life.

posts.title: Transitioning to Adult IBD Care: ImproveCareNow Transfer Toolkit
posts.description: Pediatric to adult gastroenterology handoff, self-advocacy, and skills for young adults with Crohn's or colitis. ICN Transfer Toolkit overview.
posts.share: ICN Transfer Toolkit: moving from pediatric to adult IBD care.
posts.icn_source_title: Transition to Adult Care - Transfer Toolkit
posts.body:
Young adults with IBD eventually transfer from pediatric to adult-centered care. The ImproveCareNow Transfer Toolkit is a 10-page document co-created by the Transition-Transfer Innovation Community, reviewed by the Patient Advisory Council and Clinical Practice Committee.

Why transition planning matters

Healthcare teams want young adults to navigate the system independently. Transition should feel seamless and happen when a patient is physically and emotionally stable, not only because they aged out of pediatrics.

Inside the toolkit

  • Skills adolescents need before transfer
  • Self-advocacy tips for appointments and insurance
  • Patient narratives with real-life encouragement
  • Considerations for clinicians and families at handoff

Download the toolkit from the ICN tools page. Pair with college planning, ICN college handbook, and visit prep checklist so you practice carrying your own medical summary.

posts.title: Life With an Ostomy: ImproveCareNow Ostomy Toolkit for Youth
posts.description: School, sports, travel, and telling friends about ostomy surgery. Overview of the ICN Ostomy Toolkit co-produced by pediatric patients.
posts.share: ICN Ostomy Toolkit: tips and stories for youth with ostomy surgery.
posts.body:
Ostomy surgery can feel frightening, but many young people thrive with support and honest information. The ImproveCareNow Ostomy Toolkit was created by the ICN Patient Advisory Council Advocacy Taskforce with the wider ICN community.

Designed for and by pediatric patients

The toolkit is for youth who have had or may need ostomy surgery, whether temporary (resting) or permanent.

Topics covered

  • School and telling friends
  • Travel and bathroom planning
  • Supplies, appliances, and troubleshooting
  • Playing sports and staying active
  • Clothing choices and body image
  • Reducing fear and building awareness

Download the full toolkit from ImproveCareNow. See also IBDPal's ostomy basics article and living with an ostomy guide.

posts.title: IBD During Holidays and Special Occasions: ICN Community Resources
posts.description: Holiday stress, eating, and inclusion when friends or family have Crohn's or colitis. ICN resources for special occasions and fasting.
posts.share: ICN resources for IBD during holidays and special occasions.
posts.body:
Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis do not pause for holidays, weddings, or religious observances. ImproveCareNow shares resources so friends and families can include loved ones with IBD in plans that actually work.

Resources on the ICN tools page

  • Holiday stress and eating (LOOP blog post)
  • What I wish you knew about IBD and holidays (blog and printable PDF)
  • Ramadan tips for fasting (with Crohn's & Colitis Foundation and South Asian IBD Alliance)

Why this matters

Invisible illnesses are often misunderstood at gatherings centered on food and long travel. Learning what someone with IBD may experience is a practical way to show support without pressuring them to eat or drink things that trigger symptoms.

Related: stress coping, alcohol and caffeine, travel with IBD, flare help hub.
